title: to view all the Upcoming Events on listing pages
description:
Background
Readers of PERE News and PERE Credit need quick access to upcoming PERE events to stay informed and engaged with the platform's activities. An upcoming events section on the RHS of article pages provides contextual, at-a-glance visibility of relevant events and drives traffic to the PEI Events site.
Pre-Requisite
Hardcoded events list (TABLE 1)
RHS panel component available on PERE News and PERE Credit article pages
Figma design sign-off
USER STORY 
AS A reader on PERE News or PERE Credit

I WANT TO see a list of upcoming PERE events displayed on the RHS of the article page

SO THAT I can click through to find out more details about each event or navigate to the full events calendar
Scope
Display a hardcoded list of upcoming PERE events in the RHS panel of article pages on PERE News and PERE Credit, which readers can view and click to access detailed information, and navigate to the full PERE events calendar.
Business Rules
IF a reader views an article page on PERE News or PERE Credit, THEN display the Upcoming Events section in the RHS panel as per design.
IF the Upcoming Events section is rendered, THEN display exactly the events listed in TABLE 1 in the order specified.
IF an event name exceeds 1 line, THEN truncate with an ellipsis (…).
IF a location exceeds 1 line, THEN truncate with an ellipsis (…).
IF a reader clicks on an event card, THEN navigate to the corresponding event details page in a new tab.
IF a reader clicks "View all", THEN navigate to 
https://www.peievents.com/en/pei-media-global-events-calendar/pere-events-calendar/
 in a new tab.
Technical Feasibility
Reuse the upcoming events list component built for Infrastructure Investor. The hardcoded data should be configurable per brand (II vs PERE). No dynamic data fetching required; the list will be manually updated as needed.
Usability / Design UX/UI
Reusable event list component, positioned in the RHS panel of PERE article pages
Each event card to display: month in abbreviated format (e.g., "Sep"), event name, location
Clear hover/click states per design
Consistent with II implementation; PERE-specific styling to follow Figma
Out of Scope
Dynamic/API-driven event data
Display order logic (hardcoded order from TABLE 1 applies)
Number-of-events display logic (show all 4 from TABLE 1)
Automatic expiry/removal of past events (manual update only)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Miro:

Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
Risk:
 List is hardcoded; any event changes require a manual code/config update.
Assumption:
 The same reusable component from II can be extended for PERE with brand-specific data.
Dependency:
 RHS panel must be available and rendered on PERE News and PERE Credit article pages.

title: Listing Page: Skeleton Layout for all Breakpoints
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX build, the listing page needs to be built in phases. This story covers building the static layout skeleton establishing the structural zones and slot placeholders , before any dynamic data rendering is introduced. 

Subsequent stories will handle the header, filters, article card rendering, RHS components, and pagination.
The skeleton must be built to accommodate dynamic content from the outset ,the page header, category pills, RHS sidebar composition, and article quantity in the listing grid will all vary depending on the section, filter state, and pagination and data returned. The layout must also be capable of switching between the Equity and Credit filters, and reflecting a different set of category pills per filter state. The layout must flex to handle all of this without breaking.
The page is made up of the following zones:
Breadcrumb slot
Page header
Equity / Credit filter
Category pills filter bar
Listing grid
RHS sidebar
Pagination
Pre-Requisite
Top-level taxonomy categories are confirmed and applied for Equity content (Fundraising, Managers, Investors, Deals, People)
Credit taxonomy alignment is complete before Credit pills are activated
Article card component is built and supports a visible strategy label (Equity / Credit)
URL path structure is confirmed per Brief 2 (/news, /news/equity, /news/credit)
Mega-menu links are configured to route to this page
USER STORY 
As a developer,

I need a static layout skeleton of the PERE listing page so that all structural zones are in place,

 built to accommodate dynamic content and filter switching, and ready to receive that content in subsequent stories.
Scope
Page layout for the listing page route
Two-column grid: LHS listing grid column + RHS sidebar column
Static placeholder zones for: breadcrumb slot, page header, Equity / Credit filter, category pills filter bar, listing grid, RHS sidebar, and pagination
Breadcrumb slot must exist in the correct position on the page but will not render any content in this story
Layout must be built to handle: a dynamic page header, switching between Equity and Credit filter states, a variable set of category pills per filter state, a variable RHS sidebar composition, and a variable number of articles in the listing grid
Responsive breakpoint structure (375px / 1024px / 1440px / 1920px)
No real data, no API calls, placeholder copy and lorem content only
Business Rules
IF the breakpoint is 1024px or above THEN the layout renders as two columns: LHS listing grid column and RHS sidebar column
IF the breakpoint is 375px THEN the RHS sidebar stacks below the LHS listing grid
IF the listing page loads THEN the following zones are present in order from top to bottom: breadcrumb slot, page header, Equity / Credit filter, category pills filter bar, listing grid + RHS sidebar, pagination
IF the breadcrumb slot is present THEN it renders empty with no visible content until the breadcrumb story is built
IF the Equity / Credit filter switches between states THEN the layout must accommodate the change without breaking or reflowing incorrectly
IF the category pills filter bar receives a different set of pills based on the active Equity / Credit filter state THEN the layout must accommodate the change without breaking or overflowing
IF the page header zone receives dynamic content THEN the layout must accommodate it without breaking or overflowing
IF the RHS sidebar receives a variable number of components THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing
IF the listing grid receives a variable number of articles THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing and reflect appropriate pagination.
Out of Scope
Breadcrumb content and rendering (separate story)
Page header content and rendering (separate story)
Equity / Credit filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Category pills filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Article card component rendering (separate story)
RHS component content and data binding (separate stories per component)
Pagination logic (separate story)
Nav and footer (already built)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Miro:
Confluence:
Glossary:
Sample test data:

UI:

Risks
RHS slot count and ordering may differ between Equity and Credit views, needs design clarification before build
Assumptions
Nav and footer are already built and will not be in scope for this story
The breadcrumb slot position is above the page header, as per design
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
AC1 -Two-column layout renders at 1024px and above
 

GIVEN I navigate to the PERE listing page

 WHEN the page loads at the 1024px, 1440px, or 1920px breakpoint

 THEN a two-column layout is rendered with an LHS listing grid column and an RHS sidebar column with no content overflow or layout breakage
AC2 -Layout stacks at 375px

IVEN I am viewing the listing page

 WHEN the page loads at the 375px breakpoint

 THEN the RHS sidebar stacks below the LHS listing grid in a single-column layout
AC3 - Breadcrumb slot is present but empty
 

GIVEN the listing page skeleton is rendered 

WHEN the page loads

THEN a breadcrumb slot is present in the correct position above the page header but renders no visible content
AC4 - Page header zone is present and handles dynamic content

 GIVEN the listing page skeleton is rendered

 WHEN the page header zone receives dynamic content 

THEN the zone accommodates it without breaking or overflowing at any breakpoint
AC5 - Equity / Credit filter zone is present and supports state switching

 GIVEN the listing page skeleton is rendered

 THEN the layout accommodates the change without breaking or reflowing incorrectly at any breakpoint

 WHEN the category pills filter bar zone receives a different set of pills

THEN the zone accommodates them without breaking or overflowing at any breakpoint
AC7 -Listing grid handles a variable number of articles

 GIVEN the two-column layout is rendered

 WHEN the listing grid zone receives a variable number of article placeholders

 THEN the zone accommodates them without breaking or overflowing at any breakpoint
AC8 - RHS sidebar handles a variable number of components
 

GIVEN the two-column layout is rendered

 WHEN the RHS sidebar zone receives a variable number of component placeholders 

THEN the zone accommodates them without breaking or overflowing at any breakpoint

title: the OBX footer to appear on content served from legacy WordPress sites
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, the site operates in a hybrid state where OBX pages are served by the Fusion Next.js front end and a proportion of pages continue to be served by the Classic WordPress codebase. While OBX pages carry the new Fusion footer, Classic pages require a separate footer implementation built within the Classic/WordPress codebase that is visually like-for-like with the Fusion footer.
This ticket covers the build of the Classic footer with the correct PERE branding, content, linking, and mobile rendering. The logic governing which pages the Classic footer conditionally renders on is addressed in a separate ticket.
Pre-Requisite
PERE OBX Fusion footer is implemented and available as reference
Figma designs for the PERE footer including mobile are signed off
Footer content and all link URLs are confirmed by product/editorial
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE Classic page 

I WANT to see a footer that is visually consistent with the OBX Fusion footer 

SO THAT my experience feels seamless whether I am on an OBX or Classic page
Scope
Build the Classic footer within the Classic/WordPress codebase, visually matching the OBX Fusion footer. This ticket covers the build, content, linking, and mobile rendering of the footer. Conditional rendering logic is out of scope and covered in a separate ticket.
Business Rules
BR1: Visibility
 IF a user visits any PERE Classic page, THEN the footer must be visible and visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer.
BR2: Sync with OBX Fusion Footer
 IF the OBX Fusion footer is updated, THEN the Classic footer must be updated to match.
BR3: Link Behaviour — External
 IF a footer link routes to an external URL, THEN it must open in a new tab.
BR4: Link Behaviour — Internal
 IF a footer link routes to an internal PERE page, THEN it must open in the same tab.
BR5: Mobile Rendering
 IF a user views the footer on a mobile viewport, THEN the footer must render per Figma mobile designs.
BR6: Missing Content
 IF content is not received for the footer, THEN the footer must not be rendered and all other page components must be unaffected.
Technical Feasibility
The Fusion footer component will be reused from II and adapted for the Classic/WordPress codebase.
Usability / Design
UI: The Classic footer must be visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer, matching layout, typography, colour tokens, and branding per Figma across desktop and mobile.
UX: Footer behaviour must be consistent with the OBX Fusion footer across all supported viewports:
XL: 1024px and 1440px
2XL: 1920px
Mobile
NFR / CFR
Analytics: Heap event IDs must be applied to all clickable footer elements.
Out of Scope
Fusion build
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma:  

 
UX:
UI:
MOBILE COLLAPSED VIEW
EXPANDED VIEW MOBILE
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Identify Assumptions, risks, and dependencies during requirements gathering for use cases and user stories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Footer renders on all PERE Classic pages
 

GIVEN a user visits any PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads THEN the footer is visible at the bottom of the page
AC2: Footer is visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer

 GIVEN the footer is rendered on a PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads THEN the footer matches the OBX Fusion footer in layout, typography, colour tokens, and branding per Figma
AC3: Footer updates to match OBX Fusion footer changes
GIVEN the OBX Fusion footer has been updated WHEN the Classic footer is next rendered THEN it reflects the updated design to remain like-for-like
AC4: External links open in a new tab

 GIVEN a footer link routes to an external URL WHEN a user clicks the link THEN the destination opens in a new tab
AC5: Internal links open in the same tab

 GIVEN a footer link routes to an internal PERE page WHEN a user clicks the link THEN the destination opens in the same tab
AC6: Footer renders correctly on mobile

 GIVEN a user views a PERE Classic page on a mobile viewport WHEN the page loads THEN the footer renders per Figma mobile designs
AC8: Heap events fire on all clickable footer elements
 

GIVEN the footer is rendered WHEN a user clicks any clickable element within the footer THEN a Heap event fires for that interaction
AC9: Footer renders correctly across all supported breakpoints
 

GIVEN a user views a PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads at XL (1024px, 1440px), 2XL (1920px), or mobile viewports THEN the footer layout is correct and consistent with the OBX Fusion footer at each breakpoint
Classic Footer/nScope1.Build a legacy top nav for OBX PERE�built within the Classic/WordPress codebase.2.When any user requested content is displayed from the legacy PERE or PERE Credit WordPress site, the rendered page must show the OBX footer.Will we need to decide the list of post/Pages that need to be served from legacy?/n-/nST3 DEPENDENCY

PERE (Private Equity Real Estate) is migrating from the legacy UI to the new Fusion design. 
Pre-Requisite

PERE Figma footer design signed off
USER STORY 
As a 
visitor to the PERE site
,

 across the PERE site

 So that I can

BR3: All Other Footer Elements
 

IF the footer is rendered on a PERE page, THEN all other columns, links, and bottom bar elements must be identical to the II footer.
Technical Feasibility
The Fusion footer component will be reused from II with brand-specific nav configuration applied for PERE.
Usability / Design
The footer must match the Fusion footer spec: dark background, multi-column grid, bottom bar with social and legal links.
The footer must be responsive across all breakpoints, matching II behaviour:

title: to see PERE links on the classic top nav
description:
Background
The PERE OBX site (
pei-privaterealestate.com
) includes a top navigation bar with six primary nav items, each opening a mega menu. This ticket documents the linking behaviour for all nav items  which labels are clickable, where they route, and which URLs are yet to be confirmed.
When a user interacts with the top navigation, they need to be taken to the correct destination — or understand that a label is a grouping header only — so they can navigate the site without confusion or dead ends.
Pre-Requisite
Nav bar component built (OBX Fusion and/or Classic build tickets)
URL structure for PERE News and PERE Credit confirmed and live in the relevant environment
USER STORY 
As a site visitor, I want the top navigation links to take me to the correct pages, so that I can find the content I am looking for quickly and without errors.
Scope
This ticket covers the link destinations and click behaviour for all primary nav labels and mega menu items in the PERE OBX top navigation.
Business Rules
All link destinations and clickability rules are defined in the linking table below. The following rules apply globally across the nav:
IF a label is marked as not clickable in the table, THEN clicking it does not navigate — it opens or organises the mega menu only, and no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
IF a label has a confirmed URL in the table, THEN clicking it navigates the user to that URL within the same tab
IF a label is marked TBC in the table, THEN the destination must be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development
IF the URL is an external domain (

 ), THEN the link opens in a new tab
Out of Scope
Nav bar visual design and component build
Mega menu open/close behaviour
Mobile navigation behaviour
Footer links
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Sample test data:

Risks:
 A number of URLs are marked TBC — if these are not confirmed before development begins, placeholder links or broken routes may go live.
Assumptions:
 All TBC items will be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development. The Deals item under News intentionally routes to an external domain (

 ) and opens in a new tab.
Issues:
 Healthcare under Sectors routes to 
/credit/
 rather than 
/equity/
 — this needs explicit sign-off.
Dependencies:
 URL structure decisions for Data, Performance & Rankings, and Network & Events sections.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
Functionality
GIVEN a user opens the mega menu under any primary nav item, WHEN they click a label marked as not clickable in the linking table, THEN no navigation occurs
GIVEN a user clicks any item with a confirmed URL in the linking table, WHEN the link is activated, THEN they are taken to the correct destination
GIVEN a user clicks Deals under News & Analysis, WHEN the link is activated, THEN 

  opens in a new tab
GIVEN all TBC URLs have been confirmed, WHEN the ticket is signed off, THEN the linking table is updated with final destinations before development begins
UX Behaviour
GIVEN a user hovers over a label marked as not clickable, THEN no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
GIVEN a user hovers over a clickable menu item, THEN a standard hover state is applied consistent with the nav design

title: to see a PERE logo and labels on the classic Top Nav
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, the site operates in a hybrid state where OBX pages are served by the Fusion Next.js front end and a proportion of pages continue to be served by the Classic WordPress codebase. While OBX pages carry the new Fusion nav bar, Classic pages require a separate nav bar implementation built within the Classic/WordPress codebase that matches the Fusion nav bar exactly.
This ticket covers the build of the Classic nav bar with the correct PEI PERE logo, primary nav labels, and mega menu content. The logic governing which pages the Classic nav conditionally renders on is addressed in a separate ticket. Linking is also addressed in a separate ticket.
USER STORY 
As a visitor to a PERE Classic page, 

I want to see a nav bar with PERE-relevant navigation items and branded structure,

 So that I can navigate the site consistently whether I am on an OBX or Classic page.
Scope
Build the Classic nav bar within the Classic/WordPress codebase matching the Fusion nav bar exactly. This ticket covers the logo, primary nav labels, mega menu content, and utility bar only. Conditional rendering logic and linking are out of scope.
Business Rules
IF
 a page is served by the Classic WordPress codebase, 
THEN
 the Classic nav bar implementation must be available to render on that page.
IF
 the Classic nav bar renders, 
THEN
 its layout, visual appearance, and content structure must match the Fusion nav bar exactly.
IF
 a user hovers over a primary nav item, 
THEN
 its mega menu must open and any previously open mega menu must close.
IF
 a user clicks any clickable nav element, 
THEN
 a Heap event must fire for that interaction.
IF
 the conditional rendering logic governing which Classic pages the nav bar renders on is out of scope for this ticket, 
THEN
 it must be addressed in a separate ticket.
Technical Feasibility 
UI: The Classic nav bar must match the Fusion nav bar exactly, including the dark background, PERE brand typography, logo placement, and mega menu layout per Figma
UX: Mega menus open on hover and close on moving out, consistent with Fusion nav bar behaviour
Analytics: Heap event IDs to be implemented on the PEI PERE logo and all clickable primary nav items and mega menu links
Breakpoints: Desktop
Out of Scope
Conditional rendering logic governing which pages the Classic nav renders on (separate ticket)
URL linking for any nav item (separate Classic linking ticket)
Mobile hamburger menu
OBX Fusion nav bar (covered in separate tickets)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Classic nav bar renders with correct structure and branding
 

GIVEN the Classic nav bar has been built

 WHEN it renders on a page

 THEN it displays the PEI PERE logo in the top-left, a primary nav rail displaying News & analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions, and the utility bar on the right containing Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, and the user account icon, and it matches the Fusion nav bar exactly
AC2: All primary nav items render with a mega menu

 GIVEN the Classic nav bar has been built 

WHEN a user hovers over or activates any primary nav item 

THEN a mega menu opens for that item displaying the correct columns and sub-items as confirmed in Figma
AC3: Sub-items with descriptions render description text beneath the label
 

GIVEN a mega menu is open and conta

ins sub-items with descriptions

 WHEN the user views those sub-items THEN the description text renders directly below the sub-item label in a visually subordinate style
AC4: Sub-items without descriptions render label only

 GIVEN a mega menu is open and contains sub-items without descriptions

 WHEN the user views those sub-items THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text beneath it
AC5: Only one mega menu is open at a time
 GIVEN a user has one mega menu open WHEN they hover over or activate a different primary nav item THEN the previously open mega menu closes and the newly activated mega menu opens
AC6: Heap event IDs fire on all clickable nav elements

title: to see PERE links on the top nav
description:
Background
The PERE OBX site (
pei-privaterealestate.com
) includes a top navigation bar with six primary nav items, each opening a mega menu. This ticket documents the linking behaviour for all nav items  which labels are clickable, where they route, and which URLs are yet to be confirmed.
When a user interacts with the top navigation, they need to be taken to the correct destination — or understand that a label is a grouping header only — so they can navigate the site without confusion or dead ends.
Pre-Requisite
Nav bar component built (OBX Fusion and/or Classic build tickets)
URL structure for PERE News and PERE Credit confirmed and live in the relevant environment
USER STORY 
As a site visitor, I want the top navigation links to take me to the correct pages, so that I can find the content I am looking for quickly and without errors.
Scope
This ticket covers the link destinations and click behaviour for all primary nav labels and mega menu items in the PERE OBX top navigation.
Business Rules
All link destinations and clickability rules are defined in the linking table below. The following rules apply globally across the nav:
IF a label is marked as not clickable in the table, THEN clicking it does not navigate — it opens or organises the mega menu only, and no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
IF a label has a confirmed URL in the table, THEN clicking it navigates the user to that URL within the same tab
IF a label is marked TBC in the table, THEN the destination must be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development
IF the URL is an external domain (

 ), THEN the link opens in a new tab
Out of Scope
Nav bar visual design and component build
Mega menu open/close behaviour
Mobile navigation behaviour
Footer links
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
Miro:
Confluence:
Glossary:
Sample test data:
UX:
UI:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Risks:
 A number of URLs are marked TBC — if these are not confirmed before development begins, placeholder links or broken routes may go live.
Assumptions:
 All TBC items will be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development. The Deals item under News intentionally routes to an external domain (

 ) and opens in a new tab.
Issues:
 Healthcare under Sectors routes to 
/credit/
 rather than 
/equity/
 — this needs explicit sign-off.
Dependencies:
 URL structure decisions for Data, Performance & Rankings, and Network & Events sections.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
Functionality
GIVEN a user opens the mega menu under any primary nav item, WHEN they click a label marked as not clickable in the linking table, THEN no navigation occurs
GIVEN a user clicks any item with a confirmed URL in the linking table, WHEN the link is activated, THEN they are taken to the correct destination
GIVEN a user clicks Deals under News & Analysis, WHEN the link is activated, THEN 

  opens in a new tab
GIVEN all TBC URLs have been confirmed, WHEN the ticket is signed off, THEN the linking table is updated with final destinations before development begins
UX Behaviour
GIVEN a user hovers over a label marked as not clickable, THEN no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
GIVEN a user hovers over a clickable menu item, THEN a standard hover state is applied consistent with the nav design

title: To see PERE labels on the top nav [FUSION]
description:
Background
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, PERE is migrating from the legacy Classic site to the Fusion design system. 

Following the implementation of the nav bar shell and logo, this ticket populates the

PERE SPECIFIC Top uitility Icons

Primary nav rail and mega menu dropdowns with the correct PERE-specific labels, sub-items, and descriptions as shown in the Figma designs and target state screenshots.

The II nav bar provides a reference implementation; this ticket configures PERE-specific content in place of II content
Pre-Requisite

   is complete.
PERE Figma nav designs are signed off, including all mega menu column content and featured panel
Final nav label copy is confirmed by editorial/product.
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to the PERE site

I WANT to see a PERE Specific Nav Bar

SO THAT I can understand the PERE content offering and navigate to the right section
Scope
Populate the  nav bar with correct PERE specific labels and ensure the Nav bar matches the figma from both UI (Topograhy, Spacing, colors etc) and UX(On hover, On click etc)
Top utility bar
Primary nav items
Mega Menu and descriptions
Business Rules
Top Utility Bar:IF a user visits any PERE page, THEN the following Top utility Bar Icons must be visible
Ask PEI
Search
Sign In
Register
Watchlist
Memberships
Request Demo
Notifications
User Account
Primary Nav: 
IF a user visits any PERE page, THEN the primary Nav bar must be visible displaying six top-level items: News & analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions. (As per Primary Navigation Column in Table)
IF a primary nav item has a mega menu, THEN a chevron indicator renders next to the primary nav label to indicate it is expandable.
IF a primary nav item has a mega menu, THEN hovering over or activating that item opens the mega menu and the chevron points upwards
If a mega menu is opened, any other open mega menu must close automatically.
Mega Menu
IF a mega menu column has a section heading (e.g. News, Market analysis, Investor insight), THEN the heading renders with an arrow indicator if clickable. (
Mega Menu- Section Heading )
Mega menu option as per Table. 
Supporting description
IF a mega menu sub-item has a supporting description, THEN the description renders directly beneath the sub-item label in a lighter weight.(
As per description column in Table )
IF a mega menu sub-item does not have a supporting description (e.g. items under the News column), THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text beneath it.
Technical Feasibility 
.
The mega menu slot structure supports the column count and featured panel required by the PERE nav designs 
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: All labels, descriptions, and column headings must match Figma exactly in terms of typography weight, size, and colour.
UX:
 Mega menus open on hover and close on moving outside consistent with II behaviour.
Mega menu on hover behaviour to match Figma
Column layout must not break at any supported desktop breakpoint.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Heap event IDs to be implemented on all clickable primary nav items and mega menu links.
Out of Scope
URL linking for any nav item 
Mobile Implementation
Rendering on Classic
Changes to II nav bar labels or content
Top utility bar icons based on user type.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

UX: 
PERE (MVP v2)
 
UI: 

 
Miro:
UX: 
PERE (MVP v2)

PRIMARY NAV WITH TOP UTILITY BAR 
MEGA MENUS
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Mega menu column structure from Ticket 1 supports the number of columns required by the PERE News & analysis dropdown without further structural changes. 
There is a dependency on editorial/product confirming the final label copy for all six primary nav items and all mega menu sub-items before development can complete this ticket. It is assumed that sub-item descriptions are static copy and do not require CMS management.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Top Utility Bar visibility
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN the page loads

 THEN the Top Utility Bar renders and displays the following icons in order: Ask PEI, Search, Sign In, Register, Watchlist, Memberships, Request Demo, Notifications, User Account
AC2: Primary nav renders with correct items

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN the page loads 

THEN the primary nav bar renders with exactly six top-level items in the following order: News & Analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions
AC3: Chevron indicator on expandable nav items

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

 WHEN a primary nav item has an associated mega menu

THEN a chevron icon renders adjacent to that nav item's label
AC4: Mega menu opens on hover/activation
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN a user hovers over or activates a primary nav item that has an associated mega menu 

THEN the corresponding mega menu opens and the chevron points upwards.
AC5: Only one mega menu open at a time
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and one mega menu is already open

WHEN a user hovers over or activates a different primary nav item with a mega menu

 THEN the previously open mega menu closes and the new one opens
AC6: Section heading renders with arrow indicator when clickable
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN the mega menu column contains a clickable section heading (e.g. News, Market Analysis, Investor Insight)

 THEN the heading renders with an arrow indicator
AC7: Mega menu sub-item with supporting description

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open 

WHEN a sub-item has a supporting description 

THEN the description renders directly beneath the sub-item label in a lighter typographic weight
AC8: Mega menu sub-item without supporting description

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN a sub-item has no supporting description (e.g. items listed under the News column) 

THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text below it
AC9: Mega menu content matches Figma
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN the user reviews the layout and content

 THEN all labels, descriptions, section headings, and column structure match the Figma designs exactly in terms of copy, typography weight, size, and colour

AND in terms of on hover, on click behaviour.
AC10: Mega menu closes when focus moves outside
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open 

WHEN the user moves focus or cursor outside the nav and mega menu area 

THEN the mega menu closes
AC11: Layout integrity across breakpoints

 GIVEN a OBX PERE page loads

 WHEN the user views the page at 1024px, 1440px, or 1920px viewport width

THEN the Nav bar is responsive.
AC12: Heap event tracking on nav interactions

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

 WHEN the user clicks any primary nav item or mega menu link

 THEN the corresponding Heap event ID is triggered as per the tracking specification

title: to see a PERE logo on the Top Nav
description:
Background
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, PERE is migrating from the legacy Classic site to the new Fusion design system. The top navigation bar is the primary wayfinding element across all pages and must be implemented on PERE with the correct brand identity and structural scaffolding before labels and links are applied in subsequent tickets.
This ticket covers implementing the nav bar shell on PERE: the component structure and the PEI PERE logo, 

The following to replicate as per II,  the top utility bar (Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, user account), and the primary nav rail with the correct number of top-level items as placeholders. It does not include final labels or live URLs, which are addressed in separate tickets.
Pre-Requisite
Fusion nav bar component is implemented and available in the shared component library (reference: II nav bar implementation)
PEI PERE logo asset is available and signed off by design
PERE brand colour tokens are configured in the Fusion design system
Figma designs for the PERE nav bar structure are signed off
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to the PERE site

I WANT to see a correctly structured and branded nav bar on page load

SO THAT I can identify the publication and access utility features from any page on the site
Scope
Implement the Fusion nav bar component on PERE with:
PEI PERE logo in the top-left position
Dark background nav bar consistent with PERE brand.
Mega menu shell structure per top-level item (column layout and featured panel slot, to be populated in the labels ticket)
To remain same as II for this ticket
Top utility bar
Primary nav with six top-level item slots rendered as structural placeholders (labels and links to be added in subsequent tickets)
Business Rules
IF the user visits any OBX PERE page, THEN the nav bar must render at the top of the page across all page templates
IF the page is a PEREpage THEN the PEI PERE logo must appear in the top-left of the nav bar
IF the user clicks the PEI PERE logo THEN the user must be navigated to the PERE homepage 
(Temporary destination: 
https://pereportfolio-fusion.dev.pei-services.com/
)
IF the Fusion nav bar component is already in use on Infrastructure Investor THEN the same shared component must be reused on PERE and a separate component must not be created
The mega menu slot structure should support the column count required by the PERE nav designs 
The Fusion nav bar component used on II must be reused for PERE via a brand configuration prop or config file ,a separate nav bar component must not be created for PERE. 
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: Nav bar renders with a dark background, PERE brand typography, and the PEI PERE logo at the correct size and position, per Figma. 
Utility icons render at correct size with appropriate spacing. 
UX: Nav bar is fixed/sticky at the top of the viewport. 
On hover, on click behaviour etc. to match design specifications
Responsive behaviour at desktop breakpoints  is consistent with II nav bar behaviour.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Analytics: Heap event ID to be implemented on the PEI PERE logo
Out of Scope
Navigation labels and dropdown content
Internal and external URL linking
Implementation for mobile
Rendering on Classic
Changes to II nav bar configuration
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

PERE (MVP v2)
 -UX prototype
UI: 

 
LOGO SVG: 
Miro:
UX:
UI: (TO BE RECIEVED]
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
It is assumed that the Fusion nav bar component used on II is sufficiently configurable to support PERE brand. 
Assumption

 Utility bar icon set (Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, user account) is the same component set used on II with no structural changes required for PERE.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 – Nav Bar Renders Across All PERE Pages

 GIVEN a user visits any PERE page

 WHEN the page loads

THEN the nav bar renders at the top of the page across all page templates
AC2 – PERE Logo Displays and Links Correctly

 GIVEN the page is a PERE-branded page

WHEN the nav bar renders THEN the PEI PERE logo appears in the top-left of the nav bar

 AND clicking the logo navigates the user to the PERE homepage
AC3 – Fusion Nav Bar Component is Reused

GIVEN the Fusion nav bar component is already in use on Infrastructure Investor

 WHEN the nav bar is implemented on PERE

THEN the same shared component is reused and no separate component is created

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence | Equity-Fund Performance-10 largest Real estate funds
description:
Background

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence | Equity-Fund Performance-Recent fund performance
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX , we're building the Fund Performance tab under the Equity pill of the Targeted Intelligence section. We're adding the "Recent Fund Performance" component to help GP users gain insight into fund performance for Real Estate.
This is the PERE equivalent of the II implementation delivered via:
WEB-24113
 — List Recent Fund Performance
WEB-24116
 — Fund Profile Link
WEB-24117
 — See all funds with performance data (CTA)
WEB-24115
 — Data Source LP/GP Profile Link
This story covers all 4 aspects for PERE in a single ticket.
Pre-Requisite
Domain of PERE needs to be setup and it must be up and running
Across stage, dev and prod
Targeted Intelligence section + Equity pill + Fund Performance tab UI is in place
USER STORY 
As a
 GP user 
I want
 to view most recent Real Estate fund performance from the PERE homepage 
So that
 I can analyse fund performance and conduct competitor analysis
Scope
 
To build the "Recent Fund Performance" component on the PERE OBX homepage under Equity > Fund Performance tab, covering:
Listing the 10 most recent fund performance entries from the latest quarter
Hyperlink on fund name → PERE Classic fund profile (performance history tab)
Hyperlink on Data Source institution → PERE Classic GP or LP profile (relevant tab)
"See all funds with performance data" CTA → PERE Classic fund listing page (pre-filtered)
Business Rules
Component & columns
Component labelled as 
"Recent Fund Performance"
, positioned as the second component in the Fund Performance tab under the Equity pill
Column headers displayed:
Fund — fund name
DPI — multiple format (e.g. 1.6x)
TVPI — multiple format (e.g. 1.13x)
IRR — percentage format (e.g. 8.41%)
Vintage year - year format (e.g. 2017)
Data Source — institution name (only the institution that reported the latest performance data)
Order of display
 
Only list 
10 funds
 in the component 
Only funds with performance data from the 
latest quarter
 are listed 
Ordered alphabetically (A–Z)
 
Ordered by Latest reported
Fund criteria
 (only include funds matching all of below) 
Fund Status = Closed
Fund Stage = Final Close 
Fund Asset Class = 
Real Estate
 
(PERE equivalent of II's "Infrastructure")
 
Institution Partner Status = GP, GP Fund Focus = 
PERE / RE
, 
AND 
IsActive = True 
Fund's Reported Performance 
must
 have a Data Source. 
It should not be empty
Fund hyperlink
 
All funds listed are hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the fund name, 
THEN 
they are navigated to the fund profile on PERE Classic AND land on the 
Performance History tab
 
Fund profile opens in 
the same page
Data source hyperlink
 
All institutions listed in the Data Source column are hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the institution name 
AND 
the institution is a GP
THEN 
they are navigated to the GP profile on PERE Classic 
AND 
land on the 
Funds Managed tab
 
IF 
user clicks the institution name 
AND 
the institution is an LP
THEN 
they are navigated to the LP profile on PERE Classic AND land on the 
Fund Commitments tab
 
"See all funds with performance data" CTA
 
The text "See all funds with performance data" is displayed at the bottom of the component, hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the link, 
THEN 
they are navigated to the Fund tab on PERE Classic 
AND 
view a 
pre-filtered list of funds
:
"Only funds with performance data" filter applied
Results table ordered by "Size' (Desc order)
The pre-filter logic only applies when navigating from this CTA — not when arriving at the Fund tab through other routes
Rules:
The pre-filter is same as II, except for PERE-Equity, the strategy filter should be everything except Debt

 
CTA destination opens in the same page
No regression
 
No regression to existing II Recent Fund Performance behaviour (per WEB-24113, WEB-24115, WEB-24116, WEB-24117)
UI to be same as II
Out of Scope
Applying global filters to the Recent Fund Performance component
Persistent filters (strategy, region & sector) on the Fund Listing page
Adding a performance filter to the PERE Fund Listing page if not already present — handled by ST3 
(see RAID open question) - cannot be tested util rewrite is developed
Credit pill equivalent — covered in separate story
10 Largest Real Estate Funds — covered in 
WEB-28739
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma:

 
UI:
PERE classic site links
See all redirection - 
Funds | Database | PERE
Fund redirection (e.g. Blackstone) - Funds|Performance history
Data source redirection - 
Blackstone | GP | Manager Provided Overview
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Open question for Tech -
 
Does the PERE Classic Fund Listing page already have the "Only funds with performance data" filter (equivalent of II's WEB-24052), or does ST3 need a new ticket to add it on the PERE Classic side? The CTA in this story depends on landing on a pre-filtered fund list — if the filter doesn't exist on PERE Classic, this is a dependency
Assumption - 
Since the above cannot be found out until redirection is in place (ST11 task), we would make an assumption that there would be no ST3 work on this
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — View Recent Fund Performance component
Given
 that I am a GP user 
When
 I click the Equity pill and select the Fund Performance tab 
Then
 I should see the Recent Fund Performance component 
And
 the columns Fund, DPI, TVPI, IRR, and Data Source are displayed 
And
 the component lists only 10 funds with performance data from the latest quarter 
And
 funds are ordered Size(desc order)
AC2 — Fund criteria applied
Given
 AC1 
When
 the Recent Fund Performance component renders 
Then
 only funds matching all of the following criteria are listed: Fund Status = Closed, Fund Stage = Final Close, Fund Asset Class = Real Estate, Institution Partner Status = GP with GP Fund Focus = PERE / RE and IsActive = True 
And
 the fund's reported performance must have a Data Source (not empty)
AC3 — Navigate to Fund Profile (Performance History tab)
Given
 AC1 
When
 I click a fund's name in the table 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Performance History tab on the fund profile (PERE Classic) 
And
 the page opens in the same page 
AC4 — Navigate to GP Profile from Data Source
Given
 AC1 
And
 the institution listed in the Data Source column is a GP 
When
 I click the institution name in the table 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Funds Managed tab on the GP profile (PERE Classic) 
And
 the page opens in the same page
 
AC5 — Navigate to LP Profile from Data Source
Given
 AC1 
And
 the institution listed in the Data Source column is an LP
When
 I click the institution name in the table 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Fund Commitments tab on the LP profile (PERE Classic) 

And
 the page opens in the same page 
AC6 — Navigate to "See all funds with performance data"
Given
 AC1 
When
 I click the "See all funds with performance data" link at the bottom of the component 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Fund tab on PERE Classic 
And
 the "Only funds with performance data" filter is pre-applied
And
 the results table is ordered by Size (Desc order)
And
 the page opens in the same page
Test case— No regression to existing II behaviour
Given
 the existing II homepage is rendered 
When
 the Recent Fund Performance component renders 
Then
 it continues to function as per WEB-24113 / WEB-24115 / WEB-24116 / WEB-24117 with no regression
UX
AC1
 — Build to be responsive to mentioned resolutions 
AC2
 — Hover behaviour same as II

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence > Equity-Fundraising Intelligence-Latest fundraising news > integration for latest news
description:
Background
The Latest Fundraising News component on the Equity > Fundraising Intelligence tab currently renders mock data. This story integrates the backend data layer (built in the separate backend story) with the existing frontend so the component surfaces live fundraising articles from the PERE WordPress source.
Pre-Requisite
Backend story for Latest Fundraising News must be complete
"OBX Primary" admin tag
 must be available on PERE News WordPress
USER STORY 
As a
 logged-in or logged-out user 
I want
 the Fundraising Intelligence tab to display real news articles from the editorial content platform 
So that
 I can access the latest relevant fundraising news directly from the homepage
Scope
 
Integrate the backend data fetch with the Latest Fundraising News component on the Equity > Fundraising Intelligence tab so it surfaces live articles from PERE News WordPress.
Business Rules
Content sourcing
The section must fetch the latest articles from the WordPress Fundraising category
Each article must include all the data points needed to render the card (headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and any associated tags / categories)
Rendering
 
The section renders 1 primary news card and up to 5 secondary news cards (6 total)
Primary card displays: headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and tag badges
Secondary cards display: headline, publication date, reading time, and tag badges
When fewer than 6 articles are available, only the available articles are rendered with no empty placeholders or errors
If only 1 article - showcase only 1 card on left
Fall back image - if no articles have image - Design to provide
Error & empty states
If no articles are available for the category, the section renders an appropriate empty state - same as II - Error message to be shown.
Navigation
Each news card navigates to the correct article detail page
“See all fundraising news" CTA destination — Equity > Fundraising
Out of Scope
Backend data fetch layer (covered in separate backend story)
UI changes — UI is already built
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike reference - 

 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Dependency:
 "OBX Primary" admin tag must be deployed PERE News WordPress
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Articles fetched from correct source
 
GIVEN 
the Latest Fundraising News component is rendered on the PERE homepage 
WHEN 
the section loads 
THEN 
the latest Fundraising articles are fetched from the PERE News WordPress source 
AND 
each article includes the data points required to render the card (headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, tags) 
AND 
the same logic applies on the II homepage, with articles fetched from the II source
AC2 — Rendering
 
GIVEN 
articles are returned 
WHEN 
the section renders
THEN 
1 primary card displays headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and tag badges
AND 
up to 5 secondary cards display headline, publication date, reading time, and tag badges 
AND 
when fewer than 6 articles are returned, only the available articles render with no empty placeholders or errors
AC3 — Error and empty states
 
GIVEN 
the content source returns an error 
WHEN 
the section attempts to render 
THEN 
a graceful error state is shown with no unhandled exceptions 
AND 
GIVEN no articles are returned for the category, an appropriate empty state is rendered - 

 to check on the api error for these sections
AC4 — Navigation
 
GIVEN 
any news card is rendered 
WHEN 
the user clicks the card 
THEN 
the user is navigated to the correct article detail page 
AND 
GIVEN the user clicks the "See all" footer link, the user is navigated to the Equity > Fundraising news listing page

title: PERE OBX > News & Analysis > integration for equity, credit and deals
description:
Background
This story integrates the backend data layer (built in the separate backend story) with the News & Analysis frontend component on the PERE OBX homepage. On completion, the Equity, Credit, and Deals columns will surface live articles from their respective WordPress sources.
Pre-Requisite
News & Analysis UI is already built
Backend story for News & Analysis must be complete
 (data fetch layer accepts brand context and returns articles per column)
"
OBX Primary" admin tag must be enabled
 on the relevant WordPress sources so editors can curate which articles surface in this section
USER STORY 
As a 
logged-in / logged-out user 
I want 
to be able to view the latest equity, credit and deals news on the homepage 
So that 
I can stay up to date with the latest news across all three asset classes
Scope
 
Integrate the backend data layer with the News & Analysis frontend component so that each column (Equity, Credit, Deals) renders live articles from its respective WordPress source.
Business Rules
Equity column renders articles fetched from PERE Equity WordPress source
Credit column renders articles fetched from PERE Credit WordPress source
Deals column renders articles fetched from PERE Deals WordPress source
"See all" CTA at the bottom of each column links to the Landing page which is currently being worked by other team (Navigation will not be a part of this story) - only individual article click is a part of navigation
Each card click navigates the user to the relevant article detail page
Rules-
Articles within each column are sorted by publication date (most recent first)
Each card displays: category tag, headline, read time, publication date, and thumbnail image on the first card only (as per UI)
Out of Scope
Backend data fetch layer (covered in separate backend story)
UI changes — UI is already built
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike reference: 

 
UI: 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Dependency:
 "OBX Primary" admin tag must be deployed and editors must have tagged content on the relevant WordPress sources before integration can be tested with real content

AC1 — Each column renders live articles from its respective source
 
GIVEN 
a user navigates to the PERE OBX homepage 
WHEN 
the News & Analysis section is rendered 
THEN 
the Equity column displays the latest articles from PERE Equity WordPress source 
AND 
the Credit column displays the latest articles from PERE Credit WordPress source 
AND 
the Deals column displays the latest articles from PERE Deals WordPress source 
AND 
articles in each column are sorted by publication date (most recent first)
AC2 — Article cards display correct data
 
GIVEN 
an article card is rendered in any column 
WHEN 
the user views the card 
THEN 
the card displays category tag, headline, read time, and publication date from the live article data
AND 
the first card in each column displays a thumbnail image (featured image)
AC3 — "See all" CTA navigates to correct destination
 
GIVEN 
any column is rendered 
WHEN 
the user clicks the "See all" CTA at the bottom of the column 
THEN 
the user is navigated to the Landing page (applies to landing page) - deals as well
AC3 — Article card click navigates to detail page
GIVEN 
an article card is rendered in any column 
WHEN 
the user clicks the card 
THEN 
the user is navigated to the article detail page
Test case 1 
- New & analysis to be rendered to logged-out and logged-in users

title: PERE OBX > Homepage > Bento Box > integration for Data Snapshot
description:
Background
The Data Room bento box on the homepage currently renders mock data. This story integrates the backend data layer (built in the separate backend story) with the existing frontend so the component surfaces the latest Data Room article from the PERE WordPress source.

the Data Room bento box to display the latest data visualization article from the editorial content platform 
So that

Integrate the backend data fetch with the Data Room bento box component so it surfaces the most recent Data Room article from PERE WordPress.

the content source returns an error

the bento box attempts to render
THEN 
a graceful error state is shown with no unhandled exceptions

the Data Room bento box is rendered

description:
Background
This story wires up the 
real-time counts
 for each card in the data ribbon — 
Network members (static for now) - 

 to check with ST10, Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, Service providers — sourced from the relevant APIs with brand context = 
PERE
.

 II implementation 
delivered via:

 — Fund Managers card
WEB-24765
 — Funds card
WEB-24766
 — Commitments card
WEB-24768
 — Deals card
WEB-26489
 — Service providers card

Across stage, dev and prod

WEB-28450
)

As a 
logged-in PERE user 
I want

So that 
I can quickly access PERE-specific data points

Wire up the backend + integration layer for the PERE Data Ribbon to display 
real-time counts

Funds
Commitments
Deals
Investors
Managers

Count display

Counts must be formatted with comma separators for readability (e.g. "4,368", "5,478", "11,125") - Same as II
Service providers card displays the sum of Investment Consultants + Placement Agents + Law Firms (same logic as II per WEB-26489)
Brand context
 
5. All API calls must pass brand context = 
PERE
 so the count returned is PERE-specific (not II)

6. Each card must be clickable in its entirety (not just the chevron icon) and hyperlinked to the corresponding PERE Classic destination:

 → PERE Classic Commitments tab on the investor database

LP | Database | PERE
Managers
 → PERE Classic GP database listing page

Service providers
 → PERE Classic Investment Consultants tab

Network members
 → 
Member directory of PERE
All click-through destinations open in the 
same tab
Loading state

Error state
 
10. If the API returns an error for any card, the card must remain visible (not hidden) and display an error message - same as II
Out of Scope
Data Ribbon UI changes

 a user navigates to the PERE OBX homepage 
When
 the page loads 
Then
 the Data Ribbon displays the live counts for all 7 cards (
Network members
, Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, Service providers)

brand=pere

And

 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon

 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Network members
 card 
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Network members destination on PERE Classic  
And
 the destination opens in the same tab

Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Funds
 card

Deals
 card

Investors
 card

 the API call for any card is in progress

 the data ribbon renders

title: [QA] OBX PERE - Registration Confirmation Page - Verify PERE Logo, Page Header , Footer Links, H Tags  & Form submission flow
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story
As a  
product manager,
I want
 to ensure the Registration confirmation page on OBX PERE is correctly branded and all navigational elements are in place
So that 
the page is ready for users.
Test Case Scope 
To verify that the Registration Confirmation Page is implemented in the PERE brand
To verify the PERE logo, Page Header and Footer links are correctly rendered on the OBX PERE Registration confirmation  page.
To Verify that when an user successfully submits the registration form , they are navigated to the registration confirmation page.
To verify Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration confirmation form.
Out of scope
The CTA navigation in the page header. This will be verified once the CTAs are linked by the respective team during implementation.
The primary navigation dropdown menu items and linking. This will be verified once added by the respective team during implementation.
Footer menu items and linking. This will be verified once added by the respective team during implementation.
Business Rules:  
Verify that the Registration Confirmation Page is implemented in the PERE brand for OBX PERE site.
Verify that the PERE logo is displayed correctly in the page header
Verify that the page header is rendered correctly.
Verify that the primary navigation menu is displayed and accessible.
Verify that the footer and all associated elements are rendered correctly.
Verify that when an user successfully submits the registration form , they are navigated to the registration confirmation page.
Verify the  Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration Confirmation form.
H1
 We’ve created your account
P
 To activate your account please verify your email address.
p
 A link has been sent to 
<email address>.
UX/UI
OBX PERE > Registration confirmation page
OBX PERE > Registration confirmation 
Prototype > 
PERE (MVP v2) - Protoype
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Verify Header, Logo, and Footer on Registration Confirmation Page
GIVEN 
I am an anonymous user on the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I successfully submit my registration form
AND 
taken to the registration confirmation page
THEN 
I should see the page header displayed correctly
AND 
the PERE logo visible within the header
AND 
the footer displayed correctly at the bottom of the page
AND 
the H tags are added to the Registration confirmation page as defined by WAAC.

Scenario 2- Verify registration form submission flow
GIVEN 
I am an anonymous user on the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I successfully submit my registration form
THEN 
I should be taken to the registration confirmation page which displays correct messaging.

title: OBX PERE - Marketing Landing Page - Hero Section - Product Video/Image, Existing Subscribers, Client Endorsements Sections & Hero CTAs
description:
Background 
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, we are introducing a marketing landing page for the OBX PERE site, with its own dedicated URL to showcase PEI's value proposition, product range and available subscriptions. To build this marketing page, we will be using a tool called 
Builder.io
.
User Story 
As a
 PEI Marketer, 
I want 
the Hero Section to be displayed on the marketing landing page, 
So that 
customers can can instantly grasp the main message and engage with content.
Scope 
To import the following assets into 
Builder.io
 for the OBX PERE marketing landing page.
Hero Section - Heading and description , Request a Demo CTA with CTA linking , Explore the Product CTA with CTA linking and Option to upload a video or static image with an embedded video player
Customer Base Section - Text, count and avatars
Client Endorsement Section - Text and client logos
To provide the marketing team the ability to edit or update all assets and copy within the Hero, Customer Base and Client Endorsement sections directly in 
Builder.io
.
Out of Scope 
All other sections of the marketing landing page will be handled in separate tickets.
Business Rules
Hero Section
Ensure the hero section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the hero section heading is implemented with a H1 Tag and with the text: "
Smarter, faster private real estate investment decision making"
 
Ensure the description in the hero section reads as "
Whether you’re a manager, investor or service provider, PEI Private Real Estate delivers unique private market insights to guide better, more impactful business decisions.
"
Ensure the Request a Demo CTA renders on the marketing landing page and is linked to the OBX PERE /request-demo/ page.
Ensure the Explore the Product CTA renders on the marketing landing page and navigates users to the OBX PERE Homepage when clicked.
Hero Section - Media Upload
Ensure 
Builder.io
 provides an option to upload either a video file or a static image to the hero section, with the ability to change the selected media at any time.
Ensure only one media type (video or static image) is displayed in the hero section at any given time, retaining the correct dimensions, quality and formatting as defined in Figma when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure that if a video is uploaded, a video player is rendered in the hero section; if a static image is uploaded, it displays at the correct dimensions and styling as defined in Figma.
Note: Adding a video or image to the hero section is optional. The video will be recorded and uploaded by the marketing team.
Customer Base Section
Ensure the customer base section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the text in this section reads 
"Join 16,000+ private real estate professionals".
Client Endorsement Section
Ensure the client endorsement section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the text in this section reads
 "Trusted by".
Ensure the following client logos are displayed in this section :
BGO
GIC
Brookfield
KKR
Blackstone
Ensure this Figma link is used for the import 
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
Ensure the marketing team is able to edit and update all assets and copy within the Hero, Customer Base and Client Endorsement sections directly in 
Builder.io
.
Ensure data test ids are added to the Hero Section, Media upload, Customer base and Client endorsement sections
UX / UI 
OBX PERE Marketing Landing Page > Hero Section, Embedded Media, Customer Base & Client Endorsements
Assets - 

 
Figma Link >
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations 
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Hero Section Rendered Correctly 
GIVEN
 I am a PEI marketer,
AND
 the hero section has been successfully imported from Figma into 
Builder.io
,
WHEN
 I navigate to the OBX PERE marketing landing page,
THEN
 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND
 the heading and description should be displayed as mentioned 
AND 
the heading should be implemented with a 
<h1>
 Tag

 the Request a Demo and Explore the Product CTAs should be displayed and linked correctly

 the customer base section has been successfully imported from Figma into

,

 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND
 the text, count and avatars should be displayed as mentioned.

 I am a PEI marketer,

 the client endorsement section has been successfully imported from Figma into 
Builder.io
,

 I navigate to the OBX PERE marketing landing page,

 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,

 the text and client logos should be displayed as mentioned

title: OBX PERE - Registrations (Anonymous Users) - Create Account Activation Email
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.

 marketer

So that 
it displays the correct PERE branding.

To create a new email template for user registration on OBX PERE site.
To configure the new email template with the OBX PERE site.
New email template for registration
IF a prospect successfully submits the registration form on OBX PERE and a free account is created via Zephr/CRM, THEN the prospect should receive an email using the new template prompting them to take the next steps.
Ensure the new email template is configured with the OBX PERE site.
Ensure the registration-in email is sent using the new template with the following details:
Sender email:  
no-reply@peimedia.com
Email subject: “Welcome! Activate your new account”
Brand logo / branding: PEI PERE
Email body
The email should display the user’s first name. 
( Example :  “Hello Jasper”)
Email description as 
“ Welcome to PERE! Please activate your account to begin exploring our platform”
“Activate my account” CTA ( Static link - < 
OBX PERE
> 
/auth/
activate )
Ensure that if the user clicks the “Activate my account” CTA in the email, the user should be authenticated and navigated to the auth sign-in page on the OBX PERE homepage in a new tab.

OBX PERE > Registration Email Template
Figma Link > 
https://www.figma.com/design/vZl7jzgmLoA9tYPVAqt3Jf/Registration?node-id=2045-6932&t=wlDaO9voe5zqOn7n-0

None

 the registration process completes,

title: OBX PERE & OBX II- Request a Demo Submission Confirmation Page (Registered Base & Lite Users) - Update Copy
description:
Background
We’ve introduced a new ‘Request a Demo’ experience on the Fusion pages across OBX II and OBX PERE, with an improved user interface to enable prospects to submit demo requests and allow the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. As part of this, new submission confirmation pages have also been introduced. For Lite and Base registered users who click the ‘Request a Demo’ CTA, the new fusion pages will see the new OBX Request a Demo submission registered users confirmation page, ensuring a more consistent and seamless user experience across both brands.
Scope 
To display updated confirmation messaging for registered users (free lite and paid base/base trial) on the Request a Demo submission confirmation page across OBX PERE and OBX II.
This applies only to “Request a Demo” CTAs clicked on the new Fusion pages including the Demo CTA on network components and editorial pages
Out of scope
Replace classic submission confirmation banner with new fusion confirmation page. This is handled 

 
Business Rules:
 
Ensure this messaging is updated to both OBX PERE and OBX II brand demo confirmation pages.
Ensure this update is applied only to CTAs from the new Fusion pages including the Demo CTA on network components and editorial pages
IF a user is a registered free lite user OR a registered paid base/base trial user, AND they click the “Request a Demo” CTA on Fusion pages, THEN the user should be navigated directly to the submission confirmation page without displaying the form.
Submission confirmation page - 
https://www.infrastructureinvestor.com/request-demo/confirmation/
 
This page should open in the same tab.
Ensure existing Zephr web activities/hooks are triggered to send the demo request to the CRM end.
No changes should be made to the existing OBX Fusion submission confirmation message for anonymous users. This has already been implemented. 
Please Note: The request a demo form does not be displayed for already registered users as their details already exist in our system. This is as per existing existing behaviour. 
OBX Fusion Request a Demo Confirmation Submission Page (Lite & Base / Base Trial Users)
Heading: display the text 
‘Request received’ 
Description: display the text 
'A member of our team will be in touch shortly to schedule your consultation.'
Refer to design below. 
UX/UI
OBX II > Demo confirmation page for registered users( free lite and paid base/base trial)
OBX PERE >  Demo confirmation page for registered users (free lite and paid base/base trial)

None

GIVEN 
I am a registered lite, base, or base trial user
AND 
I am logged into the OBX Infrastructure Investor (OBX II) site
WHEN 
I click on the “Request a Demo” CTA on a Fusion pages
THEN 
I should be navigated to the OBX Fusion request a demo submission confirmation page in the same tab
AND 
the registered user messaging should be displayed
AND 
the Request a Demo form should not be displayed
AND 
the existing Zephr web activities/hooks should trigger the downstream demo request process
Scenario 2 - OBX PERE: Display Request a Demo Confirmation Page for Signed-in Users (Registered - Lite, Base & Base Trial)

the registered user messaging should be displayed

the Request a Demo form should not be displayed

the existing Zephr web activities/hooks should trigger the downstream demo request process

title: OBX PERE - Request a Demo Page (Anonymous Users)- Zephr Linking to PERE Web Activities / Hooks
description:
Background
We are introducing a new Request a Demo page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit demo requests and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX Fusion infrastructure site.

the request a demo form to be configured for OBX PERE

anonymous users

).

=

checkbox is checked/true.

 the Boolean 
pere_demo_request_at_registration
 should be populated in the user attribute in Zephr

the web activity is associated only with the PERE product

title: PERE OBX Ask PEI Homescreen UI
description:
Background
The Ask PEI interface on PERE OBX displays a subheader beneath the Ask PEI logo. As PERE OBX is a private equity real estate focused platform, the subheader copy must reflect the correct brand context rather than using the II OBX copy.
User Story
AS A
 user accessing Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 the sub-header beneath the Ask PEI logo to accurately describe the feature in the context of PERE OBX,

SO THAT
 I have a clear understanding of what Ask PEI is when I open it on the PERE OBX platform.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to update the subheader copy beneath the Ask PEI logo on PERE OBX to read "AI assistant for private real estate" and to add the link to the updated privacy policy <link TBC>.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the Ask PEI subheader on II OBX
Any changes to the Ask PEI logo
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic
Any other copy changes within Ask PEI beyond the subheader
Business Rules:
The subheader beneath the Ask PEI logo on PERE OBX must read "AI assistant for private real estate".

Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
UX/UI

 Screenshot provided as design reference. The subheader sits directly beneath the Ask PEI logo and must retain its existing styling with the copy updated only.

 a user opens Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the interface renders,

THEN
 the subheader beneath the Ask PEI logo reads "AI assistant for private real estate".

GIVEN
 the subheader copy has been updated on PERE OBX,

WHEN

title: Ask PEI - Implement PERE OBX replicated as in II OBX
description:
Background
Ask PEI has been enabled on PERE OBX. To ensure users receive responses relevant to PERE OBX content, the correct brand value must be sent in the Fusion API call. Without this, Ask PEI cannot differentiate between II OBX and PERE OBX and may return responses from the wrong asset class. The brand value 
pere
 must be passed in the API call when Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX.
User Story
AS A
 user interacting with Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 my queries to return results relevant to private real estate,

SO THAT
 I receive accurate and contextually correct responses from Ask PEI on the PERE OBX platform.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to ensure the correct brand value is sent in the Fusion API call when Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX, so that responses are scoped to the PERE asset class.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the Fusion API itself
Any changes to Ask PEI on II OBX
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic beyond passing the correct brand value
Business Rules:
When Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX, the brand value 
pere
 or 
pereportfolio
 must be sent in the Fusion API call, resolving to the Private Equity Real Estate asset class.
When Ask PEI is accessed from II OBX, the brand value 
ii
 or 
infraportfolio
 must continue to be sent in the Fusion API call, resolving to the Infrastructure Investor asset class.
The brand value sent must correctly differentiate between II OBX and PERE OBX to ensure users receive contextually relevant responses.
NFRs:
No regression to existing Ask PEI behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
The correct brand value must be sent consistently across all Ask PEI interactions on PERE OBX.
UX/UI
Not applicable. This is a backend integration change with no user-visible impact beyond receiving contextually correct responses.
Screenshot:
Figma Link:  
Input
Resolves to
Asset class
ii, infraportfolio
ii
Infrastructure Investor
pere, pereportfolio
pere
Private Equity Real Estate
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: PERE Brand Value Sent in Fusion API Call
GIVEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the Fusion API call is made,

THEN
 the brand value 
pere
 or 
pereportfolio
 is sent in the API call.
AC2: Ask PEI Returns PERE-Relevant Responses
GIVEN
 the correct brand value is being sent in the Fusion API call,

WHEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

THEN
 the response returned is scoped to the Private Equity Real Estate asset class and is relevant to PERE OBX content.
AC3: II OBX Brand Value Unaffected
GIVEN
 the PERE brand value has been configured for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on II OBX,

THEN
 the brand value 
ii
 or 
infraportfolio
 continues to be sent in the Fusion API call and II OBX responses are unaffected.
AC4: No Regression on II OBX
GIVEN
 the brand value change has been implemented for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 a user interacts with Ask PEI on II OBX,

THEN
 all existing Ask PEI behaviour on II OBX is unaffected.

title: [SPIKE] Analyse what all decision can be made on the edge server
description:
Solution recording -

 

Passcode: 74N#Z9=6
SPIKE outomce 
How can we make this SSR, and what decisions can be made on the edge server
Akshay: Conduct a spike to investigate how to fetch article tags (e.g., from Algolia indexes or WordPress) in the Lambda@Edge function, and assess the latency impact of making these calls. 
Akshay: Propose the Lambda@Edge solution for server-side paywall decisioning to Stuart or Doug, and confirm their agreement before proceeding with implementation.
Akshay: Determine which components and decisions can be moved to the edge/CloudFront layer and which should remain in the application layer, based on the spike findings.
Akshay: Verify if the required article data (including tags) can be fetched from Algolia indexes instead of WordPress to optimize performance.
Trade-off between the edge server and the application 
Akshay: Schedule and conduct the spike within the current sprint, as there is available bandwidth.

title: View PERE Funds in a dedicated tab in search results page
description:
Background
The PERE OBX search results page layout has been implemented [
WEB-28573: OBX PERE - Search results page - layout set up
Awaiting Estimation
 ]. 
This ticket covers the Funds tab specifically, detailing how results are displayed and presented to the user. The Funds tab has a minimal card design displaying only the fund name.
User Story
AS A
 user searching for content on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 to see Funds results displayed in a clear and structured layout,

SO THAT
 I can quickly scan and identify relevant Funds from my search.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement the results display and card design for the Funds tab on the PERE OBX search results page.
Out of Scope 
Search bar, tab structure, and pagination.
Any other search results tabs (covered in separate tickets).
Any changes to individual Fund pages.
Business Rules:
Funds is the fourth tab.
Ensure the Funds results are rendered based on the search term entered by the user
Search will be from Opensearch
Display ALL Funds of RE asset class irrespective of partner status = GP or LP.
Display ALL Funds irrespective of them having a deal or not.
IF
 user enters search term 
 Then 
find matching firms on the 
“Funds Title” 
against the RnA db
Display order sorted in alphabetic order.
Results must be displayed in a single column layout with no category grouping or filter pills.
 Maximum up to 10 results per page
 Display total number of results on the bottom left of the grid a 1 -10 of N ( when N is the total no of results)
Each result card must display:
Display the full Firms title.
The search term is highlighted in the results.
Each result card must be clickable and navigate the user to the corresponding fund page.
Display the hand cursor when user hovers on the Funds title
IF
 user clicks the Funds title 
THEN
 user is navigated to the Funds page on the legacy site (PERE news) in the same tab.
A contextual label must be displayed above the results reading "Search results for '[query]' in Funds".
NFRs:
Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
Add data test ID to the result card component.
UX/UI
Screenshot: 
NOTE
 - Funds and Firms share similar UI.
Figma Link:  

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Single Column Layout with No Category Grouping
GIVEN
 a user views the Funds tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 results are presented in a single column layout with no category filter pills or category groupings,

AND
 Maximum up to 10 results per page,

AND
 Display total number of results on the bottom left of the grid a 1 -10 of N ( when N is the total no of results),

AND 
Display order sorted in alphabetic order.
AC2: Contextual Label Displayed
GIVEN
 a user has performed a search and is viewing the Funds tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 a contextual label is shown reading "Search results for '[query]' in Funds".
AC3: Result Card Displays Correct Content
GIVEN
 results are displayed in the Funds tab,

WHEN
 a user views a result card,

THEN
 the card displays the fund name with the search term highlighted within it.
AC4: Result Card is Clickable
GIVEN
 a user views a result card in the Funds tab,

WHEN
 the user clicks on the card,

THEN
 a hand cursor is displayed when user hovers on the Funds title,

AND
 the user is navigated to the corresponding fund page in
 
the same tab.
AC5: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN
 a user views the Funds tab at >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 the result cards are correctly displayed across all supported viewports.
