title: Create and Implement Magic Link Sign In Email Templates for PERE OBX and PERE Deals
description:
Background
SSO has been integrated on PERE OBX and PERE Deals. As part of the SSO authentication flow, an email is triggered to users to enable them to sign-in. 
Currently no dedicated email templates exist for either site, meaning users may receive emails that do not reflect the correct brand.
Branded email templates must be created and implemented for both PERE OBX and PERE Deals so that users receive a clear and consistent sign in experience.
User Story
AS A
 user attempting to sign in to PERE OBX or PERE Deals via SSO,

I WANT
 to receive a branded authentication email that clearly identifies the site I am signing in to,

SO THAT
 I can sign in with confidence and am not confused by a generic or incorrectly branded email.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to design and implement SSO authentication email templates for PERE OBX and PERE Deals.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the SSO authentication flow or logic.
Email templates for any sites other than PERE OBX and PERE Deals.
Any changes to existing II OBX email templates.
Business Rules:
A dedicated SSO authentication email template must be created and implemented for PERE OBX.
A dedicated SSO authentication email template must be created and implemented for PERE Deals.
Each template must clearly identify the site the user is signing in to.
The sign in link within each email must navigate the user to the correct destination for the respective site.
NFRs:
Email templates must render correctly across major email clients.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
No design available at this stage. Email template designs for PERE OBX and PERE Deals must be completed and signed off before development begins. This is a blocker for implementation.
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Dependency - Both templates must be designed and signed off by design team before development begins.
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: PERE OBX SSO Authentication Email Triggered
 
GIVEN
 a user initiates sign in on PERE OBX via SSO,

WHEN
 the authentication email is triggered,

THEN
 the user receives the PERE OBX branded SSO authentication email.
AC2: PERE Deals SSO Authentication Email Triggered
GIVEN
 a user initiates sign in on PERE Deals via SSO,

WHEN
 the authentication email is triggered,

THEN
 the user receives the PERE Deals branded SSO authentication email.
AC3: Sign In Link Navigates to Correct Destination
GIVEN
 a user receives an SSO authentication email for PERE OBX or PERE Deals,

WHEN
 the user clicks the sign in link,

THEN
 the user is navigated to the correct sign in destination for the respective site.
AC4: Templates Correctly Branded
GIVEN
 a user receives an SSO authentication email,

WHEN
 the email is opened,

THEN
 the email clearly identifies the site the user is signing in to and is styled as per the approved design.
AC5: Templates Render Correctly
GIVEN
 an SSO authentication email has been sent,

WHEN
 the email is opened across major email clients,

THEN
 the template renders correctly with no broken layout or missing content.

title: Ask PEI - FE URL Configuration
description:
Background
AskPEI surfaces different entities - articles, institutions, and funds - that the frontend needs to link out to. As the OBX rollout progresses, brand-specific domains are being consolidated into unified URLs (e.g. 
perenews.com
, 
perecredit.com
, and 
peredeals.com
 all consolidating to a single PERE domain).
Currently, both the frontend and AskPEI are independently setting URLs. This worked for the II OBX cutover, but creates two sources of truth that will visibly break when PERE OBX goes live - because the domain consolidation means hardcoded legacy domains will immediately produce incorrect links.
Two parallel problems need solving:
Frontend rendering
 - the FE must resolve the correct canonical URL to send users to, based on stable identifiers provided by AskPEI, not hardcoded domains.
WordPress canonical URLs
 - the URL stored against each article in WordPress must reflect the post-OBX domain structure, as this data is used for downstream data exports.
The PERE OBX cutover is the forcing function. The agreed model must be in place before that migration.
User Story
AS A
 user of AskPEI,

I WANT
 to be able to click on a link to an article, institution, or fund surfaced in a response,

SO THAT
 I should taken to the correct canonical URL on the current OBX domain - regardless of which brand I am on or whether the underlying domain has recently changed.
Scope 
Requirement 1 — Frontend URL Resolver
AskPEI must pass stable identifiers (not hardcoded URLs) to the frontend. The FE (or a shared resolver layer) owns the mapping from entity reference → current canonical URL, and resolves it at render time.
The resolver must handle five entity reference types as per table 1.
Institution URLs must carry three pieces of context: institution identity, role (LP, GP, LF, PA, IC), and section. Role is not optional — section names like "Contact information" and "Related news" appear across multiple roles, so role is part of the destination identity.
When a domain changes (e.g. 
perenews.com
 → new OBX URL), the FE must render the new URL without any changes required on the AskPEI side.
Requirement 2 — WordPress Canonical URLs
Article records in WordPress must have their URL field updated as part of the OBX migration to reflect new canonical URLs. Data exports used for sales must reflect these new URLs, not legacy ones. This ensures the FE resolver and the data sales export stay aligned on a single source of truth.
Out of Scope 
Legacy URL redirect strategy during and after OBX cutover (related but separate)
Defining the exact API contract between AskPEI and the FE (open question — see below)
Business Rules:
AskPEI must never return a hardcoded legacy domain URL to the frontend
The FE resolver is the single authority for translating entity references into canonical URLs
The static reference allowlist must be bounded and reviewable — AskPEI should not be able to cite arbitrary WordPress paths outside this list
WordPress canonical URLs must be updated as part of each OBX brand migration, not after
NFRs:
The resolver must degrade gracefully if an entity reference cannot be mapped — no broken links surfaced to users
Resolver logic must be brand-agnostic and extensible to new brands without code changes per brand
The institution role/section taxonomy is expected to grow — the resolver must not rely on per-type special cases
Table 1:
Type
Stable anchor
Notes
Article
slug
URL changes when hosting domain changes under OBX
Institution
data.peimedia.com/institutions/{id}
Resolver maps id + role + section → correct branded URL
Fund
data.peimedia.com/funds/{id}
Resolver maps id → 
/database/fund-profile/id/{fund_id}/{slug}/
Static reference
Named key (e.g. 
rankings
, 
fund-performance-methodology
)
Curated allowlist of WordPress pages AskPEI may cite
Deep breakdown
{institution} + {role} + {section} + {item_id}
Extends institution pattern one level deeper
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Open question:
 What is the agreed contract between AskPEI and the FE? Specifically — does AskPEI pass a 
data.peimedia.com
 stable ID, a structured object 
{type, id, role, section}
, or something else? This must be agreed before implementation begins.
Open question:
 How are legacy URLs redirected during and after the OBX cutover? (Related but tracked separately.)
Dependency:
 WordPress canonical URL updates are assumed to be part of each brand's OBX migration runbook — needs confirmation with the content/migration team.
Assumption:
 
data.peimedia.com/institutions/{id}
 and 
data.peimedia.com/funds/{id}
 remain stable anchors through and after the OBX migration.
Acceptance Criteria
AC1:
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns an article reference in a response,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to the correct canonical article URL on the current OBX domain, not a legacy domain.
AC2: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns an institution reference with role and section context,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to the correct institution profile URL with the correct role and section path for the current brand domain.
AC3: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns a fund reference,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to 
/database/fund-profile/id/{fund_id}/{slug}/
 on the correct branded domain.
AC4: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns a static reference (e.g. 
rankings
),

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the link resolves to the correct brand-site path, and references outside the approved allowlist are not rendered as links.
AC5: 
GIVEN
 a brand domain changes as part of an OBX cutover,

WHEN
 the resolver mapping is updated for that brand,

THEN
 all AskPEI links for that brand immediately reflect the new domain — with no changes required to AskPEI itself.
AC6: 
GIVEN
 article records are migrated as part of an OBX brand cutover 
WHEN
 a data sales export is generated post-migration 
THEN
 all article canonical URLs in the export reflect the new OBX domain, not legacy brand domains
When we implemented II we hardcoded a lot of URLs in AskPEI however for real estate we will need to shift the URL generation to being controlled by the FE, particularly as URLs will be changing.
I've prepared some documentation for you. I'd appreciate your help with prioritising this work so we can get it in for PERE.

title: Users are unable to scroll through each article in the Recent News carousel component in mobile viewport breakpoints
description:
DESCRIPTION
Beta feedback has been raised regarding the chevron (›)  indicator in the Investor data panel. 

When an investor name is long enough to wrap, the chevron drops to a new line, visually detaching from the label and resembling an accordion toggle rather than a navigation link.
ENVIRONMENTS | BROWSERS VERSIONS | URLs
Stage
STEPS TO REPLICATE
Navigate to an article page containing the Recent News carousel component in 375px and 765px viewports.
Scroll to the Recent News carousel component.
Click on the scroll arrow 
EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR
The user should be able to scroll through each article in the carousal component by clicking on the scroll arrow
OBSERVED BEHAVIOUR
When the user clicks the scroll arrow, the carousel directly navigates to the last article.
Users are only able to view the first and last articles in the carousel component and are unable to access the intermediate articles.

title: First 50 Words Preview for Paywalled Articles
description: Background A spike confirmed that surfacing the first 50 words of a paywalled article is technically feasible via client-side truncation using DOMParser . The full article HTML is already delivered to the browser and the Zephr paywall decision is made client-side, so no payload changes are required. This story covers the front-end implementation. Pre-Requisite NA USER STORY As a reader who lands on a paywalled article, I want to read the first 50 words of the article body before the paywall gate, so that I can evaluate whether the content is relevant before being asked to subscribe. Scope Client-side truncation of article body content to the first 50 words using DOMParser Truncated content renders as plain text only-no images, rich media, or embeds Truncated preview displays above the existing paywall gate component Applies to paywalled articles only Business Rules IF the article is paywalled AND THEN the first 50 words of the article body should be displayed above the paywall gate, regardless of referral source (search, newsletter, direct, social, etc.) IF the article body contains fewer than 50 words, THEN the full body text is displayed IF the first 50 words contain images, embeds, rich media, infograms, podcasts, or block quotes, THEN those elements are stripped and not counted toward the word limit, only plain text is rendered. WE CURRENTLY RENDER FEATURED IMAGES should we continue to do that? If a hyperlink falls at the 50-word cut-off point, the preview extends to the end of the full linked phrase rather than cutting mid-link. eg: Word 50 lands on "latest" but " latest acquisition " is a single hyperlink. Rather than cutting after "latest" and leaving a broken link, the preview extends to include "acquisition" so the full link is intact. …the fund announced its latest acquisition … HTML tags (, ) are not counted as words , only the visible text counts toward the 50. IF the truncation point falls mid-sentence, THEN an ellipsis is appended at the end of the preview eg: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit. Donec odio. Quisque volutpat mattis eros. Nullam malesuada erat ut turpis. Suspendisse urna nibh, viverra non, semper suscipit, posuere a, pede. Lorem..." Out of Scope Define what is included/ and not included in the scope for the User story SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION Figma: RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks Assumption: Surfacing the first 50 words of a paywalled article is technically feasible via client-side truncation using DOMParser. Risk: The full article HTML remains in the browser and is accessible via dev tools or view source. This is an accepted risk of the client-side approach Googlebot is handled separately and already fixed , it will always receive the full article. This is independent of the user-facing paywall behaviour.. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality AC1: Preview displays above paywall gate GIVEN a paywalled article is displayed with the paywall gate WHEN the page loads THEN the first 50 words of visible article body text are displayed as plain text above the paywall gate AC2: Non-text elements are excluded GIVEN the article body contains any non-text elements (images, embeds, rich media, infograms, podcasts, block quotes) WHEN the preview is rendered THEN those elements are excluded from the preview and do not contribute to the word count AC3: Preview does not cut mid-sentence or mid-link GIVEN a paywalled article is displayed with the paywall gate WHEN the preview is rendered THEN the preview cuts off at 50 words with an ellipsis appended, unless the 50th word falls mid-link, in which case the preview extends to the end of the closing anchor tag before truncating AC4: Short articles display in full GIVEN the article body contains fewer than 50 words WHEN the preview is rendered THEN the full body text is displayed with no ellipsis

title: Ask PEI - Request a demo - call web activity
description:
Background
The Ask PEI paywall will include a Request a Demo CTA alongside the existing Find Out More CTA. When a user clicks Request a Demo, a web activity must be fired to the CRM and the user must be shown a confirmation page directly — without a form — since the user is already logged in and their details are known. The CRM web activity itself will be implemented by the CRM team; this ticket covers the frontend integration of calling that activity and displaying the confirmation page upon success.
User Story
AS A
 logged-in user viewing the Ask PEI paywall,

I WANT
 to be able to request a demo with a single click and see a confirmation immediately,

SO THAT
 I can express my interest in upgrading without needing to fill in a form.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to add a Request a Demo CTA to the Ask PEI paywall, call the CRM web activity when the CTA is clicked, and display a confirmation page to the user upon success.
Out of Scope
Implementation of the CRM web activity itself (owned by the CRM team)
Any form-based data collection from the user
Any changes to the paywall beyond the addition of the Request a Demo CTA
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic
Business Rules
A Request a Demo CTA must be visible on the Ask PEI paywall.
IF
 a registered user clicks on the ‘Request a Demo’ CTA from the prompt counter or paywall 
THEN
 a web activity must be triggered in the CRM using the defined endpoint confirmed by the CRM team 
AND
 upon a successful CRM web activity call, the user must be shown the confirmation page directly 
AND
 the user must not be presented with a form.
The confirmation page must be displayed without requiring the user to input any details, as the user is already logged in.
The rule to be used as configured by Jason and CRM team is - 
https://console.zephr.com/products/features/sdk/obx-ask-pei-demo-request/
. (this is on stage but not yet configured) 
NFRs:
Responsive across the 375, 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
The CRM web activity call must not block or delay the rendering of the confirmation page beyond what is necessary.
Add data test ID to the Request a Demo CTA and confirmation page components.
UX/UI
Figma Link: 

 
Connect your Figma account
UI:
DO NOT 
display the ready to start section
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
The CRM web activity endpoint and the expected request payload must be confirmed with the CRM team before development begins. This is a blocker for implementation.
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1: Request a Demo CTA Visible on Paywall
GIVEN 
a logged-in user has reached their usage limit and the paywall is displayed,

WHEN 
the user views the paywall,

THEN 
a Request a Demo CTA is visible on the paywall.
AC2: CRM Web Activity Called on Click
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA on the paywall,

WHEN 
the click is registered,

THEN 
a web activity is called to the CRM using the confirmed endpoint.
AC3: Confirmation Page Displayed on Success
GIVEN 
the CRM web activity call has completed successfully,

WHEN 
the response is received,

THEN 
the user is shown a confirmation page directly without being presented with a form.
AC4: No Form Presented to User
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA,

WHEN 
the CRM web activity is called,

THEN 
no form or data input is presented to the user at any point in the flow.
AC5: Error Handling
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA,

WHEN 
the CRM web activity call fails,

THEN 
an appropriate error state or fallback message is displayed to the user and the page does not break.
AC6: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user views the Ask PEI paywall and confirmation page at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN 
the user interacts with the Request a Demo CTA,

THEN
 the CTA, web activity call, and confirmation page function correctly and display consistently across all supported breakpoints.

title: [TECH] Logging - Authentication events are not logged (High)
description:
Problem
The following pages and components have no logging at all:
app/auth/signin/page.tsx
app/auth/signout/page.tsx
app/[brand]/registration/page.tsx
components/session/session-sync-all.tsx
 — session state changes (authenticated / unauthenticated / loading) are never logged
app/api/register/route.ts
 only logs on failure — successful registration is silent.
What needs to be done
Log login event: timestamp, auth method, user ID
Log logout event: user ID, session duration
Log registration success: user ID, brand
Log session state changes in 
session-sync-all.tsx
: previous state → new state, user ID
Ensure all auth events carry 
user.sessionId
 for journey correlation
Acceptance criteria
Login, logout, registration, and session state transitions all produce structured log entries in DataDog. Auth-related user journeys are traceable by 
@user.id
.

title: [TECH] Logging - Page view tracking is not implemented (High)
description:
Problem
There is no logging of route changes or page views. 
app/layout.tsx
 and 
app/[brand]/layout.tsx
 have no page view events. It is not possible to trace which pages a user visited, in what order, or how they navigated through the app.
DataDog RUM is initialised with 
trackUserInteractions: true
 but this only captures raw DOM events — not structured page view events tied to the app's routing.
Acceptance criteria
Every page navigation produces a structured 
page_view
 log in DataDog containing the route path and brand. A user's page sequence is reconstructable by filtering on 
@user.sessionId
.

title: [TECH] Logging - Global error boundary does not log to DataDog (High)
description:
Problem
app/global-error.tsx
 renders an 
<ErrorFallback>
 component when an uncaught runtime error occurs but never calls the logger. Uncaught errors are completely silent in DataDog — there is no alert or log entry when the app crashes for a user.
Acceptance criteria
When an uncaught runtime error occurs, a structured error log appears in DataDog with error type, message, stack trace, and affected user context.

title: [TECH] Logging - REST API calls have no structured logging (High)
description:
Scope
Structured DataDog logging must cover the 
entire request path
, not just the caller side:
[browser/SSR] → app-portfolio → /api/proxy or direct → api-fusion → upstream (OpenSearch / DynamoDB / Algolia / GraphDB / 3rd-party) → response
                    ↑                                       ↑
              caller-side logs                        server-side logs
              (currently partial)                     (partial — middleware only)
A request must produce 
correlated
 structured log entries at every hop, queryable in DataDog by a single shared trace/correlation ID.
In scope
apps/app-portfolio
 (caller side)
Three originally-flagged offenders with no logging at all:
lib/services/fetch-view-counts.ts
lib/services/fetch-dataribbon.ts
lib/services/server/zephr-decision.service.ts
Every other outbound HTTP call
 in 
app-portfolio
 (callers of 
fetchFusionApi
, 
fetchFusionAPI
, 
fetchGraphQLBaseAPI
, 
fetchBaseAPI
, raw 
fetch()
, route handlers under 
app/api/*/route.ts
).
Outbound calls must inject DataDog/OpenTelemetry trace headers so 
api-fusion
 can join the same trace.
apps/api-fusion
 (server side)
Current state:
common/middlewares/logger.middleware.ts logs every incoming request + response — exists.
common/logger/custom-logger.service.ts — pino-based exists.
common/tracing/datadog-tracer.config.ts — exists.
Gaps to close:
Field names 
don't follow DataDog standard attributes
 — current middleware emits 
method
, 
url
, 
statusCode
, 
duration: "123ms"
 (string!). Rename to 
http.method
, 
http.url
, 
http.status_code
, 
duration
 (numeric ns) + 
http.url_details.path
 etc. (full schema below). Without this, DataDog can't auto-facet, build log-based metrics, or correlate with APM.
No correlation ID accepted from upstream callers
 — middleware doesn't read 
x-request-id
 / 
x-datadog-trace-id
 / 
traceparent
 from incoming headers and doesn't bind them to the per-request log context. Without this, an 
app-portfolio
 log and the corresponding 
api-fusion
 log can't be linked.
Per-controller / per-service operation logging is inconsistent
 — middleware only captures request boundaries. Business operations inside controllers/services (e.g. transaction aggregation, dataribbon assembly, OpenSearch query dispatch) frequently log via 
console.*
 or not at all. Each meaningful operation should emit 
start
 / 
end
 logs sharing the request's trace ID.
Outbound calls from api-fusion
 (to OpenSearch, DynamoDB, Algolia, GraphDB, any 3rd-party API) lack structured per-call logs.
Any 
console.log
 / 
console.error
 in controllers, services, or interceptors must be replaced with 
CustomLoggerService
.
Cross-cutting
Both apps must emit logs using the 
same field schema
 (DataDog standard attributes) so a single Logs Explorer query works across both services.
Both apps must participate in the 
same DataDog APM trace
 for any given request.
Out of scope
Other apps (
app-main
, 
app-networks
, 
app-deals-dataentry
, 
api-networks
, 
api-dataportal
) — separate tickets if they have similar gaps.
Building DataDog dashboards/monitors on top of the new logs — follow-up once data is flowing.
Acceptance criteria
Shared field schema (DataDog standard attributes) — both apps must emit:
DataDog attribute
Required
Notes
http.method
✅
Replaces 
method
 / 
statusCode
 field collisions
http.url
✅
Sanitize sensitive query params before logging
http.url_details.host
✅
Auto-faceting per upstream
http.url_details.path
✅
Low-cardinality endpoint faceting
http.url_details.queryString
✅
Sanitized
http.status_code
✅
Numeric — replaces 
status
 / 
statusCode
http.request_id
✅
Correlation ID — see point 3 below
duration
✅
Nanoseconds (DataDog standard) 
or
 use 
duration_ms
 (numeric, not string)
network.bytes_read
recommended
Response body size
network.bytes_written
recommended
Request body size
error.message
✅ on failure
error.kind
✅ on failure
FetchError
, 
ZephrAuthError
, 
OpenSearchTimeout
, etc.
error.stack
✅ on failure
Required for DataDog Error Tracking grouping
usr.id
 
✅
Zephr session user — populates User Journey
dd.trace_id
✅
APM ↔ Logs ↔ RUM correlation
dd.span_id
✅
pei.upstream
✅
fusion-api
 / 
zephr
 / 
algolia
 / 
wordpress-graphql
 / 
opensearch
 / 
dynamodb
 / 
graphdb
pei.module
✅
Service/feature identifier (
view-counts
, 
data-ribbon
, 
transactions
, 
zephr-decision
)
pei.operation.id
✅
Per-call ID — 
${operationName}_${Date.now()}
pei.operation.name
✅
fetch_view_counts
, 
aggregate_transactions
, 
dispatch_opensearch_query
pei.outcome
✅
success
 / 
error
pei.brand
✅
PERE
 / 
II
pei.render.context
app-portfolio only
server
 / 
client
 / 
edge
pei.cache.tags
 / 
pei.cache.revalidate
 / 
pei.cache.hit
optional
Next.js cache details
pei.attempt
when retrying
Retry counter
status
 is reserved by DataDog for log severity
 (
info
/
warn
/
error
). Don't put HTTP code there. Existing 
LoggerMiddleware
 uses 
statusCode
 which is also non-standard — must rename to 
http.status_code
.
duration
 must be numeric
, not the string 
"123ms"
 currently emitted by logger.middleware.ts:32. Otherwise DataDog can't aggregate/percentile it.
Every outbound call from 
app-portfolio
 emits a 
start
 log (
info
) and an 
end
 log (
info
 on success, 
error
 on failure), sharing the same 
pei.operation.id
.
Trace correlation works end-to-end:
app-portfolio
 injects DataDog trace headers (
x-datadog-trace-id
, 
x-datadog-parent-id
, 
x-datadog-sampling-priority
, 
traceparent
) on every outbound call to 
api-fusion
. Header injection is partially scaffolded in fusion-api-data.service.ts:116-126 (browser only) — extend to server-side using the existing OpenTelemetry context from 
instrumentation.ts
.
api-fusion
 
LoggerMiddleware
 reads incoming trace headers, derives or reuses a request-scoped 
dd.trace_id
 / 
dd.span_id
 / 
http.request_id
, and binds them to a child logger that all downstream services in the request use.
Verification: a Logs Explorer query 
@dd.trace_id:<id>
 returns matching log entries from both 
service:app-portfolio
 AND 
service:api-fusion
.
api-fusion
 controllers and services emit per-operation structured logs
 (start/end with 
pei.operation.id
) for any non-trivial operation: DB queries, OpenSearch queries, Algolia queries, GraphDB queries, outbound HTTP calls to 3rd-parties. Not just request-boundary logging from the middleware.
No 
console.*
 calls remain in HTTP-handling code paths
 in either app.
apps/app-portfolio/lib/services/**
, 
apps/app-portfolio/lib/actions/**
, 
apps/app-portfolio/app/api/**
apps/api-fusion/src/modules/**
, 
apps/api-fusion/src/common/**
 (except inside 
CustomLoggerService
 itself)
Logs are visible in DataDog with the new schema
 — verified via the queries below in stage:
service:(app-portfolio OR api-fusion) @http.status_code:>=400
service:(app-portfolio OR api-fusion) @pei.upstream:fusion-api @pei.outcome:error
@dd.trace_id:<some-id>                                   # returns BOTH services' entries
service:api-fusion @pei.module:transactions @duration:>1000000000
service:app-portfolio @error.kind:FetchError
Reusable helpers are introduced (or existing ones extended):
app-portfolio
: a 
withHttpLog(log, { op, method, url }, async () => ...)
 wrapper that emits start/end + duration + DataDog-standard fields, so future services can't accidentally skip logging. Suggested location: 
apps/app-portfolio/lib/logger/with-http-logging.ts
.
api-fusion
: extend 
CustomLoggerService
 with a 
child({ traceId, requestId, ... })
 method bound by the middleware, so controllers/services can log inside the same trace context without re-plumbing IDs.

title: OBX Homepage - News & Analysis - Fix carousel autoplay
description:
Background
The News & Analysis carousel in the bento box section of the OBX II homepage currently displays 4 articles but requires manual interaction to cycle through them. To improve content discoverability and engagement, the carousel should automatically advance through articles at a defined interval, pausing when a user hovers over it to allow them to read or interact without interruption.
USER STORY 
AS A 
user visiting the OBX II homepage,

I WANT
 the News & Analysis carousel to automatically cycle through articles,

SO THAT 
I can discover content without needing to manually interact with the carousel.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement autoplay functionality on the News & Analysis carousel in the bento box section of the OBX II homepage. The carousel must advance automatically every 8 seconds and pause on hover.
Out of Scope
Any changes to the articles displayed in the carousel or their content
Any changes to the carousel design or layout
Autoplay on any other carousels on the OBX II site
Any changes to the carousel on classic WordPress pages
Business Rules: 
The carousel must automatically advance to the next article every 8 seconds.
The carousel must pause autoplay when a user hovers over it.
The carousel must resume autoplay when the user moves their cursor away from it.
Autoplay must loop continuously through all 4 articles and return to the first article after the last one is reached.
If a user manually navigates the carousel, autoplay must resume from the currently active article after the cursor leaves the carousel.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Autoplay must not cause any layout shifts or visual glitches when the carousel advances.
Out of Scope
None
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
No design changes expected. The carousel must retain its existing appearance.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks/ Open Questions
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: Carousel Advances Automatically
GIVEN 
a user visits the OBX II homepage,

WHEN 
the News & Analysis carousel is visible and no user interaction has occurred,

THEN 
the carousel automatically advances to the next article every 8 seconds.
AC2: Carousel Loops Continuously
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
the last article is reached,

THEN 
the carousel returns to the first article and continues looping without interruption.
AC3: Autoplay Pauses on Hover
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
a user hovers over the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay pauses and the current article remains visible for as long as the cursor is over the carousel.
AC4: Autoplay Resumes on Cursor Leave
GIVEN 
a user has hovered over the carousel and autoplay has paused,

WHEN 
the user moves their cursor away from the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay resumes from the currently active article.
AC5: Manual Navigation Respected
GIVEN 
a user manually navigates to a specific article in the carousel,

WHEN 
the user's cursor leaves the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay resumes from the manually selected article.
AC6: No Visual Glitches
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
the carousel advances to the next article,

THEN 
the transition occurs smoothly with no layout shifts or visual glitches.
AC7: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user views the OBX II homepage at 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

THEN 
the autoplay behaviour functions correctly and the carousel remains visually consistent across all supported breakpoints.

title: TECH - Ask PEI Optimization
description:
Background
As part of the OBX II performance improvement initiative, lazy loading is being applied across the application. The Ask PEI chat system currently loads all of its components as part of the initial page bundle, contributing significant weight regardless of whether a user interacts with it. This ticket applies lazy loading to the Ask PEI system so that its components only load when a user chooses to engage with it, with no impact on users who do not.
User Story
AS A 
user visiting OBX II,

I WANT 
the Ask PEI chat system to load only when I choose to interact with it,

SO THAT 
the initial page load is not slowed down by components I may never use.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to apply lazy loading to all identified Ask PEI components. All implementations must use the shared lazy loading infrastructure established in 

 .
Out of Scope 
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality, logic, or destination
Business Rules:
The Ask PEI Widget must be fully lazy loaded and must not be included in the initial page bundle.
The Ask PEI Dialog must only load when a user interacts with the Ask PEI entry point.
The Ask PEI Modal must load via dynamic import and must not be included in the initial page bundle.
The Ask PEI Split Wrapper and Authenticated Ask PEI Modal must lazy load, with chat hooks and utilities bundled separately from the main bundle.
The Ask PEI button must display a loading state while the Ask PEI components are loading following user interaction.
An error boundary must be in place to handle failed Ask PEI component loads.
A retry mechanism must be in place for Ask PEI load failures caused by network issues.
Users who do not interact with Ask PEI must experience no impact on page performance as a result of this change.
NFRs:
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link:  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Ask PEI Components Lazy Loaded
 
GIVEN 
a user visits any OBX II page,

WHEN
 the page initially loads,

THEN 
the Ask PEI Widget, Ask PEI Dialog, Ask PEI Modal, Ask PEI Split Wrapper, and Authenticated Ask PEI Modal are not included in the initial page bundle.
AC2: Ask PEI Loads on Interaction
GIVEN
 a user clicks the Ask PEI entry point,

WHEN 
the interaction is registered,

THEN 
the Ask PEI components begin loading and a loading state is displayed on the Ask PEI button until the chat interface is ready.
AC3: Chat Interface Load Time
GIVEN 
a user interacts with the Ask PEI entry point,

WHEN
 the Ask PEI components have loaded,

THEN 
the chat interface is ready for use within one second of the user's interaction.
AC4: Error Handling and Retry
GIVEN
 a user attempts to load the Ask PEI chat interface,

WHEN 
a component fails to load due to a network issue,

THEN 
the error boundary prevents any impact to the rest of the page and a retry mechanism attempts to reload the Ask PEI components.
AC5: No Impact on Non-Ask PEI Users
GIVEN 
a user visits an OBX II page and does not interact with Ask PEI,

WHEN
 the page loads and the user browses,

THEN 
no Ask PEI components are loaded and there is no measurable impact on page performance for that user

title: Ask PEI - Error Messaging
description:
Background
Error handling was simplified from an earlier three-state spec. Rate limiting errors have been consolidated into the generic/fallback state. The two agreed error states cover all expected failure scenarios from the Ask PEI backend API.
These states were agreed with John Anderson (backend) and communicated to the design team (Andre Livingston) for UI treatment. Designs to be attached to this ticket prior to development.
User Story
AS A
 logged-in user of Ask PEI,

I WANT
 to see a clear, user-friendly and non-technical error message when there is an issue,

SO THAT
 I can understand there is an issue.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement two standardized error message states within the Ask PEI interface to handle API and service failures gracefully.
Out of Scope 
Business Rules:
Error messages must 
never expose HTTP status codes or technical error details
 to the user
The user's 
prompt/question must be preserved
 in the input field after an error — they should be able to retry without re-typing
Both error states display 
inline within the Ask PEI response area
 — not as a page-level banner or modal
Error state UI must be 
consistent with the Ask PEI design language
 — refer to Figma designs from Andre Livingston
The 
"Try again"
 action re-submits the exact same user query to the API
If consecutive errors occur, the same error state persists — no escalation to a different message
NFRs:
Responsive across the 1024, 1440 and 1920 viewports.
Add data test id to the component.
Error State Specifications
Error State 1: 
Temporary Service Issue
HTTP codes handled: 500, 503, 408, 504
User-facing message:
"Ask PEI is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few moments."
When to display: Server errors, gateway timeouts, and service unavailability. These are transient issues where retrying is likely to succeed.
UI behaviour:
Display error message inline within the Ask PEI chat interface (replacing the loading/response state)
Include a "Try again" CTA that re-submits the user's last question
Do not clear the user's input from the prompt field
Error should be dismissible / replaced on next successful response
Error State 2:
 Generic / Fallback Error
HTTP codes handled: 400, 401, 403, 404, 429
User-facing message:
"We couldn't process your question right now. Please try again or contact support if this continues."
When to display: Bad requests, authentication failures, permission errors, not found, and rate limiting. These may or may not resolve on retry.
UI behaviour:
UI is below for the message UI structure, but the messages themselves, should be taken from this ticket. 
Display error message inline within the Ask PEI chat interface
Include a "Try again" CTA (shown as refresh on design) – Currently not possible.
Do not clear the user's input from the prompt field
Error should be dismissible / replaced on next successful response
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Temporary Service Issue displays for 5xx / timeout errors
GIVEN I have submitted a question in Ask PEI,

WHEN the API returns a 
500
, 
503
, 
408
, or 
504
 response,

THEN I see the message: 
"Ask PEI is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few moments."

AND I see a "Try again" CTA.

AND my original question remains in the input field.
AC2: Generic error displays for 4xx errors
GIVEN I have submitted a question in Ask PEI,

WHEN the API returns a 
400
, 
401
, 
403
, 
404
, or 
429
 response,

THEN I see the message: 
"We couldn't process your question right now. Please try again or contact support if this continues."

AND I see a "Try again" CTA and a "Contact support" link.

AND my original question remains in the input field.
AC3: Try again re-submits the question
GIVEN an error state is displayed,

WHEN I click "Try again",

THEN the same question is re-submitted to the API

AND the loading state is shown while the response is being processed.
AC4: No HTTP codes or technical detail visible to user
GIVEN any error state is triggered,

WHEN the error message is displayed,

THEN the user sees only the agreed user-facing message copy — no status codes, stack traces, or API error detail.
AC5: Error state is replaced on successful response
GIVEN an error state is currently displayed,

WHEN a subsequent query is submitted and returns successfully,

THEN the error message is replaced by the new successful response.
AC6: Error UI matches design specification
GIVEN either error state is triggered,

WHEN the message is displayed,

THEN the layout, typography, and component treatment match the Figma designs supplied by Andre Livingston.

title: Ask PEI - Implement source linking (in chat)
description:
Background
To increase transparency and help users verify information provided by the Ask PEI chatbot, we need to implement inline source citations within chat responses. When users hover over or click on these source links, a popover overlay should display related articles or sources, allowing users to quickly preview and access the underlying content that informed the chatbot's response.
User Story
AS A
 user reading responses from the Ask PEI chatbot, 

I WANT 
to see inline source citations and access detailed source information via a popover,

SO THAT
 I can verify the information provided and access the original sources for deeper research.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to enable the display of inline source citations within chatbot responses and provides an interactive popover overlay that shows related source article(s) when users interact with the citation links.
Business Rules:
Source citations appear as inline links within the chatbot response text.
Source links display a category/type label (e.g., "Infrastructure Investor", "News & Analysis", "Data").
A "+N" indicator shows when multiple sources are cited.
Hovering over or clicking a source link triggers a popover overlay.
The popover displays a list of related articles/sources with titles and published dates.
Users can click on articles within the popover to navigate to the full source.
The popover dismisses when the user clicks outside of it or moves away from the source link
UX / UI
Figma Link: 

 
 UI:  
Assumptions / Open Questions
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Display Inline Source Links in Chat Response
GIVEN
 the chatbot has generated a response with source citations,

WHEN
 the response is displayed in the chat interface,

THEN
 source citations should appear as inline links within the response text,

AND
 each source link should display a category/type label (e.g., "Infrastructure Investor", "News & Analysis", "Data"),

AND
 the source link styling should differentiate it from regular text as per design specifications,

AND
 source links should be positioned inline with the relevant text content.
AC2: Display Multiple Source Indicator
GIVEN
 multiple sources are cited within the response,

WHEN
 the response is displayed,

THEN
 a "+N" indicator should be shown (where N is the number of additional sources),

AND
 the indicator should be styled according to design specifications,

AND
 the indicator should be clickable/hoverable to show all related sources.
AC3: Trigger Popover on 
Hover/click?
GIVEN
 I am viewing a chat response with inline source links,

WHEN
 I hover my cursor over a source link,

THEN
 a popover overlay should appear near the source link,

AND
 the popover should remain visible while my cursor is over the link or the popover itself.
AC4: Display Source Articles in Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is triggered for a source link,

WHEN
 the popover is displayed,

THEN
 the popover should show a list of related source articles,

AND
 each article should display its title,

AND
 each article should display its publication date,

AND
 each article should display its category/source type (e.g., "News and analysis", "Data"),

AND
 the popover content should be styled according to design specifications.
AC5: Popover Positioning
GIVEN
 a popover is triggered by hovering or clicking a source link,

WHEN
 the popover is displayed,

THEN
 the popover should be positioned near the source link,

AND
 the popover should not obscure the source link or critical content,

AND
 the popover should adjust its position if it would extend beyond the viewport boundaries,

AND
 the popover positioning should follow design specifications.
AC7: Navigate to Source Article from Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is displayed with a list of source articles,

WHEN
 I click on an article title within the popover,

THEN
 I should be navigated to the full article page,

AND
 the popover should close after navigation.
AC8: Dismiss Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is currently displayed,

WHEN
 I click outside of the popover area,

THEN
 the popover should close and disappear.
